title: NOC 21331 – Geological Engineers | GTR Immigration
description: Geological Engineers under NOC 21331 are highly sought-after professionals across infrastructure, mining, petroleum, and environmental sectors in Canada. As Canada continues to invest in natural resource development, clean energy, and large-scale infrastructure projects, the demand for qualified Geological Engineers remains steady under TEER 1. For internationally trained engineers, NOC 21331 provides strong eligibility potential under Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker Program) and various Provincial Nominee Programs.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupationsTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group21 – Professional occupations in natural and applied sciencesSub-Major Group213 – Professional occupations in engineeringMinor Group2133 – Natural resources engineersNOC Code21331

duties: Geological Engineers perform some or all of the following:
Plan and coordinate geotechnical, geological, geophysical, and geohydrological investigations
Analyze data to support mining, petroleum, civil engineering, and waste management projects
Prepare recommendations for foundation design, rock excavation, grouting, and erosion control
Assess slope stability, landslide risk, and earthquake impact
Conduct groundwater flow and contamination studies
Perform mining feasibility studies and ore reserve calculations
Design computer applications for geological and geophysical analysis
Supervise engineers, technologists, and technical staff
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in geological engineering or related discipline
Master’s degree or PhD may be required for advanced roles
Licensing as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.) through provincial engineering associations
Association of Professional Engineers and Geoscientists of Alberta
Graduate from an accredited engineering program
Complete 3–4 years of supervised engineering experience
Licensure is mandatory to approve engineering drawings and practice independently.
salary_prospects: Annual salaries typically range from $57,800 to $152,700, depending on experience, sector, and province.
job_outlook: The outlook for Geological Engineers is stable, supported by:
Ongoing mining and mineral exploration projects
Engineers with experience in sustainability, environmental impact analysis, and digital geospatial modeling may experience stronger demand.
labour_market_demand: While supply and demand remain closely balanced, strong employment prospects exist in resource-driven provinces such as Alberta, British Columbia, Ontario, Quebec, Newfoundland and Labrador, and Nova Scotia.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 21331?
High earning potential in natural resource sectors
Opportunities in mining, oil & gas, environmental consulting
Career progression into senior engineering or management roles
Stable employment in regulated engineering professions
Canada’s large-scale infrastructure and natural resource economy ensures continued relevance of this profession.
